proposed_from.topic: Sharp non-equivalence between Athey-Imbens 2006 changes-in-changes and Callaway-Li 2019 group-time quantile estimators for the QTT under staggered adoption with heterogeneous treatment-timing distributions, exhibiting a concrete moment of the timing distribution at which the two estimators have opposite asymptotic sign and recovering joint point identification of the QTT under timing homogeneity
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue material is doc/API.md Q1_StaggeredMinimalBasis, which formalizes linear FWL and staggered switch-on coefficient decompositions for mean regressions. This proposal differs by defining nonlinear CDF/quantile maps, their Gateaux derivatives, and a quantile contamination index B_tau for CIC and Callaway-Li-style QTT maps. The failed eid_dist_synth_control_v1 proposal is closer in quantile language but concerns marginal-versus-copula reporting in distributional synthetic control, not a staggered QTT influence-function/Bacon decomposition.","published_axis":"GoodmanBacon2021 and SunAbraham2021 provide signed contamination decompositions for linear mean DID/event-study estimators; CallawayLi2019 and LiLin2024 provide QTT point-identification maps under copula restrictions. The gap is the derivative-level bridge: a named operational scalar B_tau(P) that decomposes nonlinear quantile/CIC contamination into cohort-time IF terms and detects an open finite-support opposite-sign class."}
seed_list: Prior work AtheyImbens2006 and CallawayLi2019 would imply their counterfactual maps are alternative routes to QTT; this seed conjectures they agree only when a timing-skew moment and copula-balance term vanish, because staggered risk-set mixing enters before quantile inversion.

Prior work LiLin2024 would predict staggered QTT can be identified under copula restrictions; this seed conjectures the Callaway-Li route has an open-class opposite-sign wedge relative to CIC, because timing-weight skew moves the counterfactual quantile across the treated quantile.

Prior work FanYu2012 would imply failure of dependence restrictions leads to bounds; this seed conjectures staggered group-time QTT bounds require latent rank-process coupling across cohorts, because two-period Frechet bounds ignore changing risk sets.

Prior work GoodmanBacon2021 would suggest staggered contamination can be decomposed into two-by-two pieces; this seed conjectures quantile/CIC contamination decomposes only after Gateaux linearization, because quantile inversion destroys ordinary negative-weight algebra.

Prior work SunAbraham2021 would imply heterogeneous dynamic effects contaminate event-study coefficients; this seed conjectures distributional event-study QTT has contamination terms indexed by influence functions, because cohort-time CDF components enter nonlinearly.

Prior work RothSantAnna2023 would predict functional-form sensitivity for mean DID; this seed conjectures monotone transformations preserve CIC ranks but can reverse staggered QTT signs, because timing aggregation and quantile subtraction do not commute.

Prior work CallawayLiOka2018 would imply two-period CQTT can be recovered with a copula restriction; this seed conjectures the coarsest staggered copula certificate is cohort-calendar specific, because one copula cannot paste across multiple risk sets.

Prior work CallawaySantAnna2021 would imply group-time objects aggregate cleanly once identified; this seed conjectures QTT aggregates are jointly point-identified from one counterfactual object only under timing homogeneity, because otherwise quantile aggregation is not linear.

Prior proposal eid_dist_synth_control_v1 would imply a plug-in quantile-difference formula may capture the relevant equality; this seed conjectures the missing object is an equality/separation test between CIC and Callaway-Li maps, because a taxonomy does not certify sign agreement.

Prior work LiLin2024 and FanYu2012 would predict either point-ID with copulas or partial-ID without them; this seed conjectures the boundary is an LP over staggered latent rank paths, because observed marginals do not summarize feasible cross-period ranks.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AtheyImbens2006 and CallawayLi2019 would imply their counterfactual maps are alternative routes to QTT; this seed conjectures they agree only when a timing-skew moment and copula-balance term vanish, because staggered risk-set mixing enters before quantile inversion.

anchor_paper.author: Athey and Imbens; Callaway and Li
anchor_paper.claim: CIC recovers counterfactual distributions; panel QTT is identified by distributional DID plus time-invariance copula stability.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Equivalence frontier, not routine extension; not blocked because it names an operational timing-skew moment and an iff comparator frontier.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from AtheyImbens2006 and CallawayLi2019 because both recover counterfactual distributions under rank/copula stability.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: neither paper studies staggered risk-set mixtures or gives a necessary-and-sufficient equality test between the two counterfactual maps.

adversarial_referee.instance: Headline needs a named operational quantity, not just equality of estimators.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patched by making timing-skew moment M_{g,t,tau}(P) the focal scalar.

adversarial_referee.instance: Necessity of M=C=0 may not follow from equality of quantiles.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Kept as Conjecture with necessity route via finite-support perturbations.

adversarial_referee.instance: Identifies one group-time QTT but not aggregate QTT family.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patched by adding joint group-time, cohort, calendar, and aggregate QTT identification.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work LiLin2024 would predict staggered QTT can be identified under copula restrictions; this seed conjectures the Callaway-Li route has an open-class opposite-sign wedge relative to CIC, because timing-weight skew moves the counterfactual quantile across the treated quantile.

anchor_paper.author: Li and Lin
anchor_paper.claim: Staggered QTT identification is shown under distributional DID and group-invariance copula assumptions, while the Callaway-Li copula route is challenging.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/open-class non-equivalence; not blocked because it targets named CIC and Callaway-Li workflows on a non-degenerate open class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from nonlinear quantile inversion because any two estimators can disagree in sign.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the seed names the timing-skew moment that certifies opposite sign and promotes the witness to an open observable class.

adversarial_referee.instance: Sign reversal may rely on a hand-tuned finite-support example.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patched with open-neighborhood statement and T=3 non-corner witness.

adversarial_referee.instance: Li-Lin may not claim equivalence to CIC.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Positioned as resolving their flagged Callaway-Li extension difficulty, not refuting an equivalence claim.

adversarial_referee.instance: Needs critical scalar for sign reversal.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Uses M_{g,t,tau}(P) with margin epsilon bounded by curvature c(P).

seed_details.one_line: Prior work FanYu2012 would imply failure of dependence restrictions leads to bounds; this seed conjectures staggered group-time QTT bounds require latent rank-process coupling across cohorts, because two-period Frechet bounds ignore changing risk sets.

anchor_paper.author: Fan and Yu
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp bounds are available for distributional and quantile DID in a simpler two-period setting.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends two-period to staggered; not blocked only if it exposes coupled-rank non-rectangularity, otherwise field-tier.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from applying Makarov/Frechet bounds cell by cell.
seed_details.rebuttal: False if shared latent ranks across cohort-period cells create non-rectangular feasible sets; needs LP witness.

adversarial_referee.instance: Needs finite LP dimension and extreme-point map.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would need LP constraints over latent rank paths; not chosen.

adversarial_referee.instance: Bounds may already be covered by later distributional DID papers.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Deepening needed; downgraded behind chosen equivalence seed.

adversarial_referee.instance: Bounds should cover QTT(g,t), aggregates, and distributional effects.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patch available but not drafted in v1.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work GoodmanBacon2021 would suggest staggered contamination can be decomposed into two-by-two pieces; this seed conjectures quantile/CIC contamination decomposes only after Gateaux linearization, because quantile inversion destroys ordinary negative-weight algebra.

anchor_paper.author: Goodman-Bacon
anchor_paper.claim: TWFE DID with timing variation decomposes into weighted two-group/two-period DID comparisons.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends decomposition to quantiles; not blocked because the extension changes the object to influence-function components and sign reversal.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just the functional delta method applied to known decompositions.
seed_details.rebuttal: Partly lands; without a corrected estimator and rate it is below the chosen seed.

adversarial_referee.instance: Decomposition without corrected QTT estimator would cap at field.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Lands unless corrected estimator E* is added; not chosen.

adversarial_referee.instance: Panel flagship requires estimator and rate.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs M9 package; not drafted.

adversarial_referee.instance: Goodman-Bacon is mean TWFE, not CIC/QTT.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use only as motif comparator, not direct theorem target.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work SunAbraham2021 would imply heterogeneous dynamic effects contaminate event-study coefficients; this seed conjectures distributional event-study QTT has contamination terms indexed by influence functions, because cohort-time CDF components enter nonlinearly.

anchor_paper.author: Sun and Abraham
anchor_paper.claim: Event-study lead and lag coefficients can be contaminated by effects from other periods under heterogeneous treatment effects.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends event-study contamination to QTT; blocked unless it produces a corrected estimator and influence-function weights.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from linearizing quantiles around Sun-Abraham coefficients.
seed_details.rebuttal: Likely too close unless paired with a new corrected estimator; downgraded.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work RothSantAnna2023 would predict functional-form sensitivity for mean DID; this seed conjectures monotone transformations preserve CIC ranks but can reverse staggered QTT signs, because timing aggregation and quantile subtraction do not commute.

anchor_paper.author: Roth and Sant'Anna
anchor_paper.claim: Mean DID parallel-trends conclusions can be sensitive to the functional form chosen for the analysis.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Functional-form variation; not blocked if it gives necessary and sufficient transformation class for sign/order preservation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate because quantiles transform equivariantly under monotone maps.
seed_details.rebuttal: False after staggered aggregation and quantile subtraction; equivariance does not imply sign/order preservation of aggregated QTT.

adversarial_referee.instance: A sufficient class of transformations would be incremental.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would require necessity; not selected for v1.

adversarial_referee.instance: Needs named transformation frontier.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Could define H_sign(P); left as lower-ranked seed.

adversarial_referee.instance: Roth-Sant'Anna may already characterize transformation sensitivity broadly.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Gap is nonlinear staggered QTT, but needs deeper check.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CallawayLiOka2018 would imply two-period CQTT can be recovered with a copula restriction; this seed conjectures the coarsest staggered copula certificate is cohort-calendar specific, because one copula cannot paste across multiple risk sets.

anchor_paper.author: Callaway, Li, and Oka
anchor_paper.claim: Two-period CQTT identification uses conditional distributional DID plus a copula restriction.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends two-period to staggered; not blocked only if it proves non-pasteability and a minimal certificate.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate because staggered designs are repeated two-period designs.
seed_details.rebuttal: False if risk sets overlap and the same latent copula must be compatible across multiple cohort-calendar contrasts.

adversarial_referee.instance: Needs coarsest certificate as named operational object.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Possible but less directly tied to topic than timing-skew moment.

adversarial_referee.instance: Non-pasteability necessity may be hard.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would need boundary counterexample; not chosen.

adversarial_referee.instance: Certificate should identify CQTT and QTT family jointly.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patch available through joint-family statement.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CallawaySantAnna2021 would imply group-time objects aggregate cleanly once identified; this seed conjectures QTT aggregates are jointly point-identified from one counterfactual object only under timing homogeneity, because otherwise quantile aggregation is not linear.

anchor_paper.author: Callaway and Sant'Anna
anchor_paper.claim: Group-time ATT parameters are the building blocks for staggered DID identification, estimation, and aggregation.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: CATE/aggregate variant risk; not blocked only if quantile nonlinearity reveals joint-ID failure hidden by mean ATT aggregation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a routine restatement that quantiles are nonlinear and do not aggregate like means.
seed_details.rebuttal: Partly lands; useful as corollary to chosen seed, not enough as standalone flagship kernel.

seed_details.one_line: Prior proposal eid_dist_synth_control_v1 would imply a plug-in quantile-difference formula may capture the relevant equality; this seed conjectures the missing object is an equality/separation test between CIC and Callaway-Li maps, because a taxonomy does not certify sign agreement.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Reviewer noted AtheyImbens2006CIC + CallawayLi2019QTEDID + FanYu2012PartialDID prior art and a plug-in quantile-difference formula.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Prior-proposal anchor; not blocked because it upgrades a taxonomy into a computable equality/separation theorem.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just repairing an old failed CausalSmith taxonomy.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the new kernel is a named timing-skew iff plus open-class sign reversal, not a taxonomy.

adversarial_referee.instance: Prior proposal failure may not be a publishable literature gap.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Cross-linked with Athey-Imbens, Callaway-Li, and Li-Lin paper anchors.

adversarial_referee.instance: Needs operational separation test.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patched by M_{g,t,tau}(P).

adversarial_referee.instance: Separation might be one hand-tuned law.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Open-class statement and T=3 witness address it.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work LiLin2024 and FanYu2012 would predict either point-ID with copulas or partial-ID without them; this seed conjectures the boundary is an LP over staggered latent rank paths, because observed marginals do not summarize feasible cross-period ranks.

anchor_paper.author: Li and Lin; Fan and Yu
anchor_paper.claim: Staggered QTT point identification uses copula assumptions; two-period distributional DID has sharp bounds without point-ID dependence restrictions.
anchor_paper.location: Li-Lin identification discussion; Fan-Yu main bounds

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Bounds/LP seed; not blocked if finite LP and extreme-point characterization is delivered.
seed_details.referee_objection: This follows from standard finite response-type LP sharpness.
seed_details.rebuttal: The staggered rank-path coupling may be new, but the standard LP objection is strong; not chosen.

adversarial_referee.instance: LP sufficiency without minimality is incremental.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would need active-rank lower bound.

adversarial_referee.instance: Computable map absent unless LP dimensions are explicit.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Not selected.

adversarial_referee.instance: Recent staggered distributional DID may already cover this.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs deeper scan before use.

proposed_from.literature_map: Athey and Imbens (2006, Econometrica, AtheyImbens2006) identifies full counterfactual distributions in nonlinear DID through stable-rank CIC maps. Callaway and Li (2019, Quantitative Economics, CallawayLi2019) identifies panel-data QTT using distributional DID plus time-invariance copula stability. Callaway, Li, and Oka (2018, Journal of Econometrics, CallawayLiOka2018) gives the two-period conditional QTT copula predecessor. Li and Lin (2024, Economics Letters, LiLin2024) extends QTT DID to staggered adoption and flags the Callaway-Li time-invariance copula route as challenging. Fan and Yu (2012, Economics Letters, FanYu2012) gives partial-ID bounds for distributional and quantile DID when point-ID dependence restrictions fail. Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021, Journal of Econometrics, CallawaySantAnna2021) supplies group-time indexing for staggered DID. Goodman-Bacon (2021), Sun-Abraham (2021), and de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille (2020) show timing heterogeneity contaminates mean DID estimators, motivating an analogous nonlinear QTT diagnostic. Roth and Sant'Anna (2023, Econometrica, RothSantAnna2023) shows DID conclusions can be functional-form sensitive, relevant to CIC versus quantile-sign comparisons. Ciaccio (2024/2025 arXiv) and recent staggered QTT work emphasize full counterfactual distribution recovery but do not give an equality frontier between CIC and Callaway-Li maps. The main open tension is nonlinear mixing: cohort timing enters before quantile inversion, so a timing-distribution moment can flip QTT signs even when primitive distributional changes look aligned.
